gate_type: capability check edit_theme_options (outer) + wp_nonce admin-customizer-only (inner) + capability check install_plugins wrapped in overrideable filter
reasoning: The exploitation chain requires multiple gates, none of which are reachable by an unauthenticated or low-privilege attacker in a default WordPress install. Gate 1 (outer, init() line 58): current_user_can('edit_theme_options') is enforced unconditionally by the WordPress Customizer itself — only authenticated users with at minimum Editor role can reach the customize_register action at all; this gate is NOT subject to the cei_allow_unsafe_imports filter. Gate 2 (nonce, _import() line 251): wp_verify_nonce($_REQUEST['cei-import'], 'cei-importing') is verified before processing any upload. Critically, the nonce is generated via wp_nonce_field('cei-importing', 'cei-import') in includes/control.php line 24, which is itself inside an 'if (CEI_Core::can_import())' block — so the nonce is only revealed to users for whom can_import() returns true. Gate 3 (inner, can_import() line 44): apply_filters('cei_allow_unsafe_imports', current_user_can('install_plugins')) is the broken gate, but it only matters after Gate 1 is already passed. The net effect of the filter bypass is a privilege escalation from edit_theme_options (Editor) to install_plugins (Administrator) within the customizer — not an unauthenticated bypass. An unauthenticated attacker cannot reach the customizer panel, cannot obtain the wp_nonce_field value, and therefore cannot trigger the _import() path regardless of any filter hook. The finding is real (a lower-privileged authenticated user such as an Editor could exploit it IF a third-party plugin carelessly hooks cei_allow_unsafe_imports), but it is NOT default-exploitable without: (a) an authenticated Editor-level account AND (b) a second component that hooks the filter. Severity is downgraded from MEDIUM to LOW because exploitation is conditional on a non-default prerequisite (the hooking third-party component) and requires existing authenticated access.
